Method​1 hour long semi-structured interviews with agent builders, focusing on data & tools. ​Topics covered:​•AI use cases​•Data sources, tools used​•Tech stack​•Pain points in agent building, in general​•Pain points with data, tools​•Perceptions of Azure, Foundry (if familiar) or similar tools​•​See discussion guide for more details​Background​Microsoft Foundry aims to be the platform on which developers shape the future with AI. Knowledge and Tools are critical to this. They allow customers to ground their agentic systems in trusted data and take real actions across systems.​​Study Goals​•Get a grounded understanding of professional developers’ use cases, JTBD, and pain points when building agentic solutions, with a particular focus on knowledge and tools.​•Consider how our offerings do/don’t meet customer needs today. What we might do to better serve them?​​Background & Goals​

Agenda​Day Zero Cloud Interviews: Neoclouds and Startup Cloud Concept​aka.ms/agxstudio​01​Background ​02​High-level findings​03​Discussion​

Day Zero Cloud Interviews: Neoclouds and Startup Cloud Concept​

Day Zero Cloud Interviews: Neoclouds and Startup Cloud Concept

Background​aka.ms/agxstudio​MICROSOFT CONFIDENTIAL​

About this Research​Day0 Cloud –  Startup Interviews​aka.ms/agxstudio​​​​This research supports Azure’s strategy to win AI-native startups at two pivotal inflection points: Day Zero Onboarding and Migration from developer-first cloud platforms. The goal is to further understand startup or “neocloud” tech stacks and the tipping points that initiate migration to a hyperscaler​9 Participants:​•AI Native Startup executives​•US-based​•4 sourced from Microsoft startups program, 5 from third party recruitment​1.Understand motivations and decision process for choosing developer first, neocloud platforms.​2.Identify constraints that push startups to consider hyperscaler migration.​3.Assess key friction points for migration.​4.Uncover core factors driving hyperscaler selection – platform capabilities, industry reputation, startup program support etc.​5.Gather feedback on startup cloud concept​6.​•Startup AI Infrastructure on Azure: Usage Patterns, Challenges, and Opportunities​•VC and SME Interviews for Day0 Cloud support​•AI Tech Stack Survey Early Results_deeper dive.pptx​Background​Research Goals​Participants​•Semi-structured In-depth Interviews​•60-minute in length​•Participants detailed their current cloud providers, described how their cloud usage evolved as their product grew, and discussed migration efforts. Finally, we discussed our concept of a startup cloud to gather their input on key features and points of hesitation.​Method​Previous Research	​

NormalNo SpacingHeading 1

Insights from a survey of 27 participants and early customer walkthroughs reveal preferences and mental models for structuring custom model training features within the Foundry platform. The research explores how users conceptualize and navigate tasks related to building, training, and fine-tuning machine learning models, aiming to optimize information architecture (IA) labeling and navigation flows.

Build as primary entry point: Customers consistently prefer "Build" as the top-level navigation label for initiating model customization tasks, aligning with their intent-driven mental model of creating or progressing model work. "Discover" serves as a complementary entry for exploratory scenarios. 1 2

Build as primary entry point:

Distinct roles of Train and Fine-Tune: Users differentiate between "Train" (full training jobs with control over code and optimization) and "Fine-Tune" (parameter-efficient, example-based improvements). Collapsing these into one option causes confusion and friction. Including both supports iterative workflows. 3 4

Distinct roles of Train and Fine-Tune:

Terminology sensitivity: Ambiguous or broad terms, especially those including "AI" (e.g., GenAI, AI Services), reduce clarity and are viewed as buzzwords by experienced users. More precise labels like "Model fine-tuning" and "Task fine-tuning" are preferred. 5 6

Terminology sensitivity:

Clear calls to action and visibility: Users expect clear CTAs to create new fine-tuning jobs and value at-a-glance information such as usage data, status indicators, and performance charts that build confidence and orientation within workflows. 7 8

Clear calls to action and visibility:

Survey navigation label findings: "Build" ranks highest as a top-level navigation label across tasks, outperforming "Customize" and "Operate," which can be ambiguous. Side-level navigation shows task-dependent preferences where "Fine-Tune" dominates for LoRA and speech-to-text tasks,while "Train" dominates for training open-source models. 9 10

Survey navigation label findings:

Feature-level labeling preferences: "Model fine-tuning" and "Task fine-tuning" emerge as favored within-feature labels. The label "Custom" is often flagged for ambiguity and is less preferred. Users think more in terms of the activity performed rather than broad service categories. 11 12

Feature-level labeling preferences:

Variant evaluation: Among tested IA variants, those combining both "Fine-Tune" and "Train" under "Build" with clear, action-oriented labels (e.g., Variant 4) are most favored, reflecting real-world iterative workflows and clearer mental model alignment. 13

Participant demographics and JTBDs: Respondents mainly consist of software and AI/ML platform engineers engaged in tasks such as training or adapting models, deploying them, and evaluating performance, using a mix of open-source foundation, traditional ML, and proprietary models. 14 15

Participant demographics and JTBDs:

Information Architecture Foundry

Custom Model Training

This writeup completes insights from our initial survey learnings, now with n=27 and shares early insights from live customer engagements evaluating select IA variants.   The goal of the survey was to understand customers’ mental models regarding the task/JTBD  of custom model training-including how customers expect these capabilities to be structured, named, and grouped within Foundry. As well as identifying which IA variant(s) held the most promise for follow-up deep-dive engagements with customers.
